steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Function question. The prior sentences state that much of the historical evidence comes from Indigenous oral traditions and from autobiographies written by freedom seekers. The phrase "One such narrative" signals that the underlined sentence will give a specific example of that kind of autobiography.

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: The underlined sentence identifies Loguen's autobiography and details specific help (food, lodging, directions) the Neshnabé villagers offered — serving as a concrete example of the general claim about autobiographies documenting Indigenous assistance.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• "It provides an example of an autobiography that describes help given by an Indigenous people to a Black freedom seeker.": accurately captures the example function signaled by "One such narrative." ✓
  • "It shows why Loguen decided to write in great detail about his experiences traveling from Tennessee to Canada in his autobiography.": the sentence reports what Loguen's autobiography contains, not his motivations for writing it — misreads the sentence. ✗
  • "It argues that autobiographies are particularly important sources of information about geography in the United States before the Civil War.": overreaches; the sentence makes no claim about geographical importance — it describes assistance received. ✗
  • "It suggests that most historians believe that Neshnabé villagers were more successful in assisting freedom seekers than other people were.": the sentence makes no comparative claim about Neshnabé villagers versus other groups — contradicts/overreaches the text. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
